description_long: This is a bulbous, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body that tapers gently toward a narrow neck and flares out at the rim. The surface displays a matte, slightly rough texture with visible color variations in earthy tones, suggesting it may have been shaped and fired using traditional pottery techniques. A single, sturdy handle is attached from the upper neck to the shoulder, providing a functional grip for pouring or carrying. The rim is uneven and subtly pinched at one side, giving the vase a rustic, artisanal character. The coloration ranges from pale beige to muted terracotta, with some areas showing lighter patches, possibly from wear or restoration. This vessel would be well-suited for decorative use in urban parks, public plazas, or as a street-side planter.
description: A rustic, bulbous ceramic vase with a single handle, matte finish, and earthy color variations. The rim is uneven, adding to its artisanal charm.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from the front, highlighting the wide opening, rounded body, and the handle attached to the left side.
description_view_1: This view shows the vase without the handle visible, emphasizing the smooth, continuous curve of the body and the pinched rim.
description_view_2: The handle is clearly visible on the right, and the body’s color variations and surface texture are prominent.
description_view_3: The back of the vase is shown, with the handle arching from the neck to the shoulder and the flared rim visible.
